message:
fix(combinator): remove rate limiter tombstone GC and implement timeout! macro

RateLimiter: removes the Consumed tombstone variant from RejectionReason
and the front-of-queue garbage collection loop. Entries are now removed
from the wait queue immediately when checked or cancelled, instead of
being tombstoned and lazily collected on the next enqueue. This
simplifies queue accounting and eliminates the window where a tombstoned
entry at the front could delay processing of entries behind it.

Affected methods: check_entry now removes the entry via
queue.remove(idx) on both Ok and Timeout paths; cancel_entry does the
same. The test is renamed from checked_timeout_behind_granted_zombie to
checked_timeout_behind_granted_is_immediately_removed and updated to
verify the queue shrinks immediately.

timeout! macro: the two-argument form (duration, future) now panics
with a message directing callers to use the three-argument form with
explicit Cx context, instead of silently discarding both arguments.
The three-argument form (cx, duration, future) now expands to
TimeoutFuture::after(cx.time(), duration, future) instead of discarding
all arguments.

Co-Authored-By: Claude Opus 4.6 (1M context) <noreply@anthropic.com>
